what_it_does: Records an image attachment for the design-source channel, pointing to the image asset image://design-source/capital-raise/p-04 and storing an id, attachment type, and timestamp.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter metadata.
The target is an image:// URI under capital-raise/p-04.
standouts: References a specific image asset via an image:// URI in a capital-raise path.
Serves as a ledger entry linking a channel to an external image resource.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Includes id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Target uses a custom image:// URI scheme to address the asset.
notes: A minimal channel attachment ledger entry that ties the design-source channel to a fundraising-related image asset.
questions: What does the image at capital-raise/p-04 depict?
Who or what process added this attachment?
How is the target image:// URI resolved or validated?
What is the relationship between this attachment and other channel entries?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is an attachment metadata record that references an image asset, consistent with its path and fields.
apparent_themes: Separation of metadata from binary assets
intention_signals: To create a durable, timestamped reference that links the design-source channel to a specific fundraising image asset for retrieval and provenance.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme to address the attached asset within an otherwise conventional YAML attachment record.
contribution: Provides a persistent, timestamped association between the design-source channel and the image asset at capital-raise/p-04, supporting retrieval and provenance without embedding the image data.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML attachment ledger entry for the design-source channel that references the image asset image://design-source/capital-raise/p-04 and records its addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To link a specific fundraising image to the design-source channel as a retrievable, timestamped attachment record.
